title: Fixing CSS Media Query Priority with Inconsistent Feature Ordering

statement.markdown: You are tasked with implementing a CSS media query parser. The parser should correctly determine the priority of the media queries based on the feature ordering. However, the existing implementation contains a logical bug that causes incorrect priority ordering. Your task is to fix this bug and provide a correct implementation.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a list of media queries in the format 'feature1: value1, feature2: value2, ...'
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a list of media queries with their priority ordering corrected
statement.notes: Note that the feature ordering is critical in determining the correct priority. The features with higher values should have higher priority.

examples.output: [{ query: 'max-width: 1024px, max-height: 768px, min-width: 320px', priority: 3 }, { query: 'min-width: 320px', priority: 2 }, { query: 'max-width: 1024px, max-height: 768px', priority: 1 }]
examples.explanation: The correct priority ordering is based on the feature values. The media query with the highest value has the highest priority.

examples.output: [{ query: 'max-width: 1024px, max-height: 768px, min-width: 320px', priority: 3 }, { query: 'min-width: 320px', priority: 2 }, { query: 'max-width: 1024px', priority: 1 }]
examples.explanation: The correct priority ordering is based on the feature values. The media query with the highest value has the highest priority.

hints.text: First hint text: The priority ordering is based on the feature values. The media query with the highest value has the highest priority.
Second hint text: The media queries with the same feature values should have the same priority.

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: Sort the media queries based on the feature values.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: Iterate through the media queries and compare their feature values.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: Use a sorting algorithm to sort the media queries based on their feature values.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: The sorting algorithm ensures that the media queries with the highest values have the highest priority.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Sort the media queries based on their feature values.
commonMistakes: Mistake 2: Failure to handle media queries with the same feature values

functionSignature.python: def parseMediaQueries(self, media_queries: list[str]) -> list[dict[str, int]]:
